META.reasoning: No valid breakout setup exists for META. The stock suffered a violent post-earnings breakdown on Apr 30, collapsing ~$57 from $669.12 to $611.91 close (intraday low $600.00) on 3.34x relative volume — institutional distribution, not a breakout catalyst. Per the wiki, the prior $675-677 breakout thesis is fully invalidated. Key issues for breakout strategy: (1) No consolidation range or base is forming — price is in active post-earnings trending decline; (2) Bollinger Band width is 0.2353 (daily) and 0.1434 (hourly) — bands are expanding post-breakdown, not squeezing; no coiled-spring setup; (3) ADX 49.21 with minus_di (34.51) > plus_di (26.27) confirms a strong bearish trending regime, not a range-bound environment that precedes breakouts; (4) Hourly ADX minus_di at 49.52 vs plus_di at 8.98 is extreme directional imbalance — active freefall, not consolidation; (5) Support at $627.61 was breached by the gap-down; the $600 hourly pivot held intraday but the stock did not close below it, so no confirmed downside breakout to trade either; (6) Nearest resistance is $618-630 dead-cat bounce zone (prior support turned resistance, Fib 38.2% at $626.10) — this is a mean-reversion target, not a breakout level; (7) Wiki warns next entry only on bounce into $618-630 before considering a short. For a bearish breakout entry to be valid, I would need: price to consolidate above $600 support for several sessions, BB width to contract back into a squeeze, then a high-volume close below $600. That setup does not exist today. Recent lesson (NVDA breakout stop triggered at Fib 61.8%) reinforces discipline around premature breakout entries in volatile post-event tape. Range boundaries identified: upper range $627-630 (resistance), lower range $600 (support). Volume ratio: 3.34x (distribution, not breakout accumulation). Squeeze status: off — already fired to the downside. No actionable setup for breakout strategy.
META.timeframe: no setup — revisit in 5-10 trading days once consolidation forms
